business.headline: India eases FDI rules for neighbouring countries: What the new 10% rule meansThe decision was approved by the Union Cabinet and the changes are intended to boost foreign investment, help startups access global funding and support manufacturing growth in India.

Why Reliance Industries shares are gaining in early trade todayAt around 9:23 a.m., RIL shares were trading at Rs 1,427.20, up Rs 18.40 or 1.31% from the previous close of Rs 1,408.80. The stock opened at Rs 1,424.90 and touched an intraday high of Rs 1,434.00.

Sensex, Nifty trade flat in early session; Reliance gains, banks weighAt around 9:18 a.m., the BSE Sensex was at 78,279.66, up 73.68 points or 0.09%. The Nifty 50 stood at 24,256.90, down 4.70 points or 0.02% after touching an intraday high of 24,292.35.

Air India fares to rise as Iran war pushes up jet fuel pricesAir India said the fuel surcharge is due to the sharp rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices since early March 2026, triggered by supply disruptions in the Gulf region.

G7 stops short of releasing oil reserves, asks IEA to assess market optionsAfter soaring to a more than three-year high in the previous session, oil prices plummeted 7 per cent on Tuesday as US President Donald Trump predicted the war in the Middle East could end soon, easing concerns about prolonged disruptions to oil supplies.

India eases FDI rules for China, allows up to 10% stake without govt approvalUnder the revised policy, overseas investors with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India will now be allowed to invest through the automatic route.

Main Hoon Naa: IndiGo interim head's message to employees amid leadership changeRahul Bhatia expressed gratitude to employees who worked tirelessly to restore operations during that challenging period, praising their resilience and dedication.

IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers steps down 3 months after operational meltdownThe resignation comes after the airline faced its worst-ever operational crisis in December, which drew scrutiny from both the public and the government.

Airlines raise fares as Iran conflict sends jet fuel prices soaringThe Iran-US conflict has disrupted shipping through a key global oil export route, pushing oil prices higher. As a result, airline tickets on some routes have become much more expensive, and there are growing concerns that travel demand could weaken if the situation continues.

No LPG crisis, production up 10% today, says government, warns against fake newsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

Middle East countries cut daily oil output amid Hormuz disruptionFour of the region's largest producers, including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait have collectively reduced output by as much as 6.7 million barrels per day.
Sensex closes 640 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; M&M gains 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 639.82 points to close at 78,205.98, while the NSE Nifty50 added 233.55 points to end at 24,261.60.

Essential Commodities Act invoked by India amid LPG shortage. What does it mean?Restaurants, hotels and commercial kitchens in cities such as Delhi, Mumbai and Bengaluru have reported difficulty getting commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to scale down operations or temporarily shut. The situation has pushed the government to invoke the Essential Commodities Act

Silver jumps nearly Rs 10,000: Will the Iran-US war push prices even higher?Geopolitical shocks are once again driving money into precious metals. Silver has surged nearly Rs 10,000 amid rising tensions between the US, Israel and Iran. As uncertainty deepens, the big question now is: can silver rally even further?

Prolonged Gulf conflict could be catastrophic for oil markets, says Aramco chiefSpeaking during a media call after the company released its 2025 financial results, Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser said the disruption represents the most serious crisis faced by the region's oil and gas sector in recent years.

EPF rate stays at 8.25%: Should investors continue with VPF or choose SIPs?For many salaried employees, EPF and Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF) are trusted tools for retirement savings. But with the EPF rate staying at 8.25%, some investors are wondering if they should keep contributing to VPF or move extra savings into SIPs for better long-term returns?

Highlights: No need to panic, India expands oil sourcing to 40 countries, say Govt sourcesLPG Shortage Highlights: A growing LPG cylinder shortage is disrupting restaurants and hotels across several Indian cities, raising concerns over a widening gas supply crisis. Hospitality associations have warned that eateries are struggling to procure commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to shut operations temporarily. The shortage is being reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, where restaurant bodies say supplies have become irregular over the past few days. The government has asked oil companies and refineries to increase LPG production to stabilise supply. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, city-wise impact on restaurants, supply disruptions, and steps being taken to address the crisis.

When crude oil hit a record $147 per barrel in 2008 and the reason was not warCrude oil briefly touched $120 amid Gulf tensions, reviving memories of the historic 2008 oil shock when prices hit $147. But unlike this conflict, the 2008 surge was not driven by war or geopolitics. So what pushed oil prices so high? Read on to find out.

Why have crude oil prices fallen below $100, and will it go up again?Crude oil prices had surged above $100 per barrel on Monday but fell today after recent comments from US President Donald Trump. Experts say that even though prices have fallen today, the risk to global oil supply has not gone away.

LPG shortage shuts 20% of Mumbai hotels and restaurants Live UpdatesThe association warned that up to 50% of hotels in the city could shut down within the next two days if the gas supply situation does not improve.

entertainment.headline: Is Prabhas set to collaborate with Eko director? New pic sparks buzz Actor Prabhas' recent meeting with Malayalam director Dinjith Ayyathan, who helmed Eko, has sparked fresh speculation about a potential collaboration. Eko was one of the critically acclaimed films of 2025.

RGV says Dhurandhar The Revenge will rattle South cinema if it earns Rs 2,000 crore Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma says Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 could disrupt the dominance of South films if it crosses the ₹1500–2000 crore mark. Here's why the filmmaker believes the sequel could reshape box office trends.

Puneeth Rajkumar's Akash: Why this 20-year-old Appu film still feels special Puneeth Rajkumar's 2005 musical hit film Akash is set to re-release in theatres on March 13. Alongside the late actor, the film stars Ramya and features a diverse music album that remains reminiscent for Kannada audiences to this day.

Rihanna-A$AP Rocky and children were home when woman fired gunshots: Attorney A woman from Florida faces multiple charges, including attempted murder, after allegedly firing a semiautomatic rifle outside the Los Angeles home of musician Rihanna and A$AP Rocky. Authorities said the couple and their children were inside the property at the time of the incident.

Best to end it here: Director Parthiban says he's closing chapter on Trisha row Filmmaker R Parthiban has decided to end the controversy with actor Trisha after his remarks at a Chennai event sparked backlash. He posted a brief apology and shared a detailed video explaining his comments and stance.

Cardi B seen with oxygen mask after Denver show, jokes about city's thin air Videos of Cardi B using an oxygen mask during her concert in Denver caught fans' attention. The rapper later addressed the moment with humour, while experts noted that high altitude can cause shortness of breath and fatigue, especially during intense physical activity.

Neeru Sehgal on playing Kohrra S2's Loveleen: Felt like being allowed to breath Actor Neeru Sehgal talked about moving from advertising to long-format storytelling with her role as Loveleen in Kohrra Season 2. She shared how performing in Punjabi, preparing for the character and working on a Netflix series shaped her experience.

Trisha-Parthiban showdown: Public spat explained in 10 points Actor Trisha and filmmaker R Parthiban are embroiled in a public dispute following the latter's controversial remarks at a Chennai award show. The exchange has drawn widespread attention and is yet to die down.

Critics' Choice Awards 2026 full nominees list: Homebound, Black Warrant take lead The 8th Critics' Choice Awards shared the full list of nominees, with Black Warrant and Homebound leading in their respective categories. The event will take place on March 24 in Mumbai.

Divya Agarwal receives rape threats from Prince Narula fans, shares abusive texts Divya Agarwal claimed she received online rape threats from fans of Prince Narula following their dispute on the show The 50. Sharing video messages, the actor also called Narula "self-obsessed."

Hannah Montana trailer: Emotional Miley Cyrus marks 20 years of Hannah-versary Miley Cyrus returns as Hannah Montana for a special anniversary programme. The anniversary special will premiere twenty years after the original show's debut, with recreated sets, interviews and unseen footage.

Our missing child: Supriya Pathak on her bond with Shahid Kapoor's wife Mira Rajput Supriya Pathak spoke about her bond with Mira Rajput Kapoor, saying she has never thought of her as a daughter-in-law but as part of the family. She also shared how her grandchildren, Misha and Zain, bring joy to their household.

Johnny Lever's daughter Jamie recalls brother Jessey's tumour battle at age 10 Jamie Lever recalled the difficult time when her brother Jessey Lever was diagnosed with a neck tumour at the age of 10. She shared how the family, including Johnny Lever, relied on faith as Jessey underwent two surgeries before he eventually recovered.

Rajpal Yadav celebrates 20 years of Malamaal Weekly, thanks fans for love The Priyadarshan comedy Malamaal Weekly was released 20 years ago on March 10. Rajpal Yadav, whose character Bajey gained immense popularity, thanked fans for all the love, sharing a compilation of his funniest scenes from the film.

Elijah Wood hints at his return as Frodo Baggins in The Hunt for Gollum Elijah Wood has said that he doesn't want anyone but himself playing Frodo Baggins in The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum. Wood played Frodo in the previous instalments of the film series.

Pankaj Kapur says asking for script made him a difficult actor early in his career Pankaj Kapur said he was labelled a "difficult actor" early in his career because he insisted on reading the script before accepting roles.

One Piece Season 2 review: It gets bigger, stranger and mostly betterRelease Date:Tue Mar 10, 2026 One Piece Season 2 is finally streaming on Netflix. The series follows Monkey D Luffy and the Straw Hats as they enter the chaotic Grand Line, facing bizarre adventures and new allies.

Rashmika-Vijay's Pradhanam pics: All about her Shiva-Shakti mehndi and his style Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda shared pictures from their mehndi and pradhanam ceremony. Other than their moments from the pre-wedding festivities, it was Rashmika's beautiful mehndi that caught the attention of fans.

Ajith Kumar returns to Chennai after being stranded in Dubai amid US-Iran conflict The actor had reportedly been in Dubai when rising tensions in the UAE disrupted international flight routes, leaving travellers stranded.

general news.headline: Will Harish Rana, in coma for 13 years, get right to die? Supreme Court verdict today UpdatesHarish Rana's parents have sought passive euthanasia after years of medical care and failed legal attempts, making the case a crucial test of India's right-to-die jurisprudence.

Iran firing fewer missiles. Have its launchers been killed or is it a game plan?With the war in its second week, Iran is firing 90% fewer missiles than in the immediate days after the initial strikes by the US and Israel on February 28. While the US and Israel peg this as their success in destroying Iran's launchers, Tehran claims fewer missiles is part of its evolving strategy. This is what we know.

Deleted post, Iran denial: Did US Navy escort oil tanker through Hormuz?In a now deleted post on X, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright claimed the US Navy had escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz to ensure global energy supplies continued flowing.

Trump announces historic $300 billion oil refinery in Texas, thanks RelianceAnnouncing the project in a Truth Social post, Trump said the refinery will be built in Brownsville, Texas, as part of a USD 300 billion deal aimed at boosting American energy production and strengthening economic ties with international partners.

Strategic patience: Iran continues to keep India close despite US, Israel tiesDespite India's close ties with Israel and the US, Iran continues to maintain a restrained and pragmatic approach towards New Delhi, recognising the strategic value of preserving a long-standing partnership built on energy ties and geopolitical balance.

Why visit only temples? Harbhajan responds to Kirti Azad's dig at Team IndiaFormer cricketer Harbhajan Singh has responded to 1983 World Cup winner Kirti Azad's question - why do the Indian team always visit a temple, and not a mosque, or a church? Harbhajan's response has gone viral.

RGV says Dhurandhar The Revenge will rattle South cinema if it earns Rs 2,000 croreFilmmaker Ram Gopal Varma says Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 could disrupt the dominance of South films if it crosses the ₹1500–2000 crore mark. Here's why the filmmaker believes the sequel could reshape box office trends.

Time, Tide & Tamil Part 31 | MGR's 'Thottam' Theatre: Politics, Power-Plays & ParadoxesMGR was a star in the stacks of history, building the infallible halo that bridged the studio floor to the secretariat with a veteran's poise.

Why climate change will empty your tap before it floods your streetClimate on My Plate is India Today Science's new series on how the climate crisis is reshaping the everyday things you eat, drink, buy and own. This week, it is the glass of water you pour without thinking.

Live: Suspected projectile attack reported on vessel near UAE as Hormuz shipping haltsUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the joint front of Israel and the United States entered its 12th day on Wednesday, with fresh Iranian missile attacks targeting central Israel before dawn as air raid sirens sounded, and Israeli air defences intercepted incoming rockets. Iranian state media also reported new strikes on US military installations in Bahrain early Wednesday, following a wave of Iranian missile and drone attacks late Tuesday on bases linked to the US in Qatar, Iraq's Kurdistan, the UAE and Bahrain. The escalation comes after intense US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran on Tuesday, described by the Pentagon and witnesses as among the heaviest of the war so far. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned it could block oil shipments from the Gulf unless the attacks stop, raising fears of disruption to global energy supplies. Meanwhile, the White House reiterated US President Donald Trump's warning of severe retaliation if Iran attempts to halt shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, even as he said US forces had destroyed 10 Iranian 'inactive' mine-laying vessels in recent strikes. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East war.

Titanic-inspired Trump-Epstein statue pops up at Washington DC's iconic Mall parkThe installation comes amid renewed scrutiny over newly released US Justice Department records linked to the Epstein investigation that include sexual assault allegations involving Trump, which the White House has categorically denied.

Tees Maar Khan: Biography of Trump as the most dangerous fool in the worldHe is not a villain. He does not scheme. He simply acts, with total conviction, on a reality he invented. And the world keeps handing him a bigger sword, even when all he knows is how to squish flies.

Why coffee badging is becoming the new workplace trend among Gen ZAs hybrid work reshapes office culture, employees are redefining what it means to "show up" at work. A new trend called coffee badging where workers briefly visit the office before leaving to work elsewhere is gaining attention across global workplaces.

India's submariners go deep. Their health data never surfacesHandcrafted ByOur Editors Sealed inside a steel tube at crushing depth, breathing manufactured air, eating off the same table where surgery will be performed if something goes wrong, India's submariners inhabit one of Earth's most hostile environments.

US turned down Ukraine's anti-Shahed drone tech months before Iran war: ReportDuring a closed-door meeting at the White House on August 18 last year, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy offered interceptor drones and related technology to the US as part of a partnership proposal, a report by Axios suggests.

25-year-old autistic US man calls 911 for wellness check, police shoot him deadAlex LaMorie, a 25-year-old autistic man in Maryland, had called 911 during a suicidal crisis seeking a wellness check. He was shot dead by police.

Rashmika-Vijay's mehendi outfits by Torani blend colour, tradition and boho charmRashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's mehendi outfits designed by Karan Torani boasted a burst of colours and rich Indian craftsmanship.

Authorities said the couple and their children were inside the property at the time of the incident.

7 Iranian women footballers receive asylum in Australia during West Asia tensionsSeven members of Iran's women's soccer team were granted humanitarian visas in Australia after seeking asylum, while the rest of the squad returned home following protests and concerns over safety amid the Iran war.

How Iran war is forcing restaurants in Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai to shutThe ripple effect of the Iran conflict and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy transit route, has led to an LPG cooking gas crunch in India. It has severely impacted the hospitality sector in Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka.

She shared how performing in Punjabi, preparing for the character and working on a Netflix series shaped her experience.

India now has over 50 living CheetahsThe birth marks a proud moment for Project Cheetah, which aims to restore the world's fastest land animal to Indian landscapes decades after it went extinct in the country.

SA, WI travel decisions made on safety: ICC rejects bias claimsT20 World Cup: The ICC on Wednesday, March 11 said that the South Africa and West Indies teams will leave India within the next 36 hours. The international cricket body also rejected claims that they showed any bias towards both sides during the travel fiasco that has seen them get stranded in India.

sports.headline: Gambhir slams Kirti Azad after T20 World Cup remark: Degrading your own players India coach Gautam Gambhir slammed Kirti Azad's comment about the team taking the T20 World Cup trophy to the Hanuman temple in Ahmedabad, saying it was degrading the players and their achievement on the day.

7 Iranian women footballers receive asylum in Australia during West Asia tensions Seven members of Iran's women's soccer team were granted humanitarian visas in Australia after seeking asylum, while the rest of the squad returned home following protests and concerns over safety amid the Iran war.

T20 World Cup medal in hand, Shivam Dube reveals how he returned home via train T20 World Cup: Just hours after helping India win the T20 World Cup, Shivam Dube quietly boarded a train from Ahmedabad to Mumbai. With flights full, the all-rounder chose the fastest route home to see his children.

Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill was great T20 World Cup call by Gambhir: Harbhajan T20 World Cup: Harbhajan Singh praised Gautam Gambhir and the selectors for backing Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill at the T20 World Cup. Kishan repaid that faith with a breakout campaign as India lifted the title.

SA, WI travel decisions made on safety: ICC rejects bias claims T20 World Cup: The ICC on Wednesday, March 11 said that the South Africa and West Indies teams will leave India within the next 36 hours. The international cricket body also rejected claims that they showed any bias towards both sides during the travel fiasco that has seen them get stranded in India.

FC Barcelona and Liverpool stunned on UCL return, Atletico thrash clueless Spurs Barcelona and Liverpool stumbled on hostile away nights in the Champions League Round of 16 first legs. Atletico Madrid punished Tottenham brutally, while Bayern Munich delivered a ruthless six-goal statement against Atalanta.

ODI World Cup, WTC wins bigger than T20 World Cup: Manjrekar odd take goes viral Former cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar has gone viral over his odd take on the T20 World Cup. Manjrekar feels that ODI World Cup and the WTC wins are bigger achievements than winning the T20 World Cup.

England right-back Kyle Walker retires 4 games short of 100 international caps Kyle Walker has announced his retirement from international football, bringing the curtain down on a 14-year England career. Walker played for England 96 times and appeared at five major tournaments.

Frustrated with ICC, Windies board arranges flights for stranded players The West Indies Cricket Board is arranging commercial flights for its players and staff who continue to be stranded in India. The CWI has taken matter into its own hands after repeated delays by the ICC.

Rishabh Pant turns to Yuvraj Singh for career-revival in IPL Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant is training under Yuvraj Singh ahead of the IPL 2026. Pant, who had a horrid time in the IPL last year, is hoping to revive his career in the T20 format.

Bangladesh vs Pakistan ODI series Live Streaming: Squads, all you need to know BAN vs PAK: Shaheen Shah Afridi's Pakistan and Mehidy Hasan Miraz's Bangladesh are all set to lock horns in a three-match ODI series to be held from March 11 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur.

Australian GP Review: Dominant Mercedes, familiar Ferrari frustrationsThe 2026 Formula 1 season began with a thrilling Australian Grand Prix, but beneath Mercedes' dominant 1-2 finish were deeper questions about the new regulations, Ferrari's strategy woes, and a promising new rookie generation.

Watch: Sanju Samson's emotional reunion with family after dream T20 World CupT20 World Cup: Sanju Samson shared an emotional reunion with his family in Trivandrum, thanking his parents and brothers for their unwavering support throughout his cricketing journey.

Why visit only temples? Harbhajan responds to Kirti Azad's dig at Team IndiaFormer cricketer Harbhajan Singh has responded to 1983 World Cup winner Kirti Azad's question - why do the Indian team always visit a temple, and not a mosque, or a church? Harbhajan's response has gone viral.

Babar Azam's future in the dark? Pakistan focus on youngsters before ODI World CupBAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi stated that Pakistan rested Babar Azam for the Bangladesh series to provide opportunities to youngsters, with next year's ODI World Cup in mind.

New Zealand's moment in the sun not far awayT20 World Cup: The New Zealand men's team has played four ICC limited-overs finals since 2000 but has ended up on the losing side each time. On Sunday, the Kiwis were defeated by India by 96 runs in a one-sided final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Manisha Kalyan's world-class free-kick breathes life into Indian footballWomen's Asia Cup 2026: Manisha Kalyan's stunning free-kick gave India a brief moment of hope, but Chinese Taipei ran out 3-1 winners in Sydney, ending India's Women's Asia Cup campaign without a single point.

Sahibzada Farhan set for ODI debut after T20 WC surge, confirms Shaheen AfridiBAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi confirmed that Sahibzada Farhan, Shamyl Hussain, and Maaz Sadaqat will make their ODI debuts against Bangladesh in the series opener on Wednesday in Mirpur.

Watch: Ishan Kishan deflects question on Kirti Azad's controversial temple remarksIshan Kishan calmly avoided commenting on Kirti Azad's controversial remarks about taking the T20 World Cup trophy to a temple after India's victory. Instead, he urged journalists to focus on India's triumph and the team's memorable campaign.

Arshdeep fined for heated T20 World Cup final bust-up with Daryl MitchellFast bowler Arshdeep Singh has been reprimanded for his heated bust-up with New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell. In the T20 World Cup final, Arshdeep chucked a ball straight into the batter's body, in the 11th over of the game.

IPL 2026 schedule for first 20 matches to be announced by March 13: BCCIThe BCCI will unveil the schedule for the first 20 days of IPL 2026 by March 13, as confirmed by Secretary Devajit Sikia. The board is releasing fixtures in phases while factoring in state election dates.

Reminiscent of Viv Richards: Gavaskar's glowing verdict on Samson's World Cup heroicsT20 World Cup 2026: Sunil Gavaskar compared Sanju Samson to Viv Richards and Mahela Jayawardene after his Player of the Tournament performance at the T20 World Cup, praising his rare purity of batting.

Hell of a career: Starc pays special tribute to Alyssa Healy on Australia farewellMitchell Starc paid tribute to his wife Alyssa Healy after her international farewell, celebrating her remarkable career following Australia's Day-Night Test win over India at the WACA in Perth.

Delhi Capitals head coach Hemang Badani finds new role in The HundredHemang Badani has been appointed head coach of Southern Brave for the 2026 season of The Hundred, with Ian Bell serving as his assistant. The Brave will start their campaign on July 22 against Welsh Fire in Southampton.

West Indies, South Africa remain stranded in India after flight home gets cancelledT20 World Cup 2026: West Indies and South Africa cricketers remain stranded in Kolkata after their charter flight arranged to take them home was cancelled due to airspace clearance issues amidst the ongoing tensions in the West Asia region.

ICC call off Doha board meetings due to West Asia chaosThe ICC has called off its scheduled board and committee meetings in Doha due to escalating tensions in West Asia. Key discussions on broadcasting rights, the next FTP cycle and Olympic qualification will now be held virtually.

Jacob Bethell rewarded for T20 World Cup semis 105, named Birmingham Phoenix captainJacob Bethell has been named captain of Birmingham Phoenix for the 2026 season of the Hundred after his standout T20 World Cup campaign, highlighted by a 105 off 48 balls against India in the semi-final.

FIFA World Cup 2026 still on track in USA despite escalating West Asia CrisisFIFA says the 2026 World Cup in the USA will go ahead despite the ongoing conflict in West Asia, with Iran's participation in the tournament remaining uncertain due to the war.

UEFA Champions League Round of 16 preview: Key clashes to look out forThe UEFA Champions League Round of 16 is set to deliver big clashes as Europe's top clubs begin the knockout battle for Budapest. Heavyweights like Real Madrid, Manchester City, PSG and Barcelona headline the ties.

Wish you were here: Rinku Singh's emotional note for father after T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup: Rinku Singh shared an emotional note for his father on social media after India won the global event on Sunday. Last month, his father passed away following a prolonged battle with stage-4 liver cancer.

Gujarat Titans rope in Matthew Hayden as batting coach for IPL 2026IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans have brought in Australia great Matthew Hayden as batting coach ahead of IPL 2026. The former opener will work with captain Shubman Gill and the Titans' powerful batting unit to sharpen their approach.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
